{{Quote|Looks like the Imperials welded an engine booster pack to a simple TIE fighter. Wow, can that thing move!|[[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] [[Gwen Habbert]]|<ref name="Star Wars: X-Wing Alliance">''[[Star Wars: X-Wing Alliance]]''</ref>}}
The '''TIE Experimental M5''', also known as the '''TIE Booster''', was an experimental member of the [[TIE series/Legends|TIE Series]] and a part of the [[TIE Experimental Project]].

==Characteristics==
One of the [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]] built by an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] research project led by [[Director/Legends|Director]] [[Lenzer]] under the command of [[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Demetrius Zaarin]], the M5 was essentially a standard [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE/LN starfighter]], but with a large booster engine attached to the aft of the fighter.

The fighter, thanks to the booster, was much faster than the standard TIE/LN, but its maneuverability was slightly lower. Like the other TIE Experimentals, the M5 was remotely controlled by a modified [[Beta-class ETR-3 escort transport|''Beta''-class ETR-3 escort transport]]. The fighter was equipped with a [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]], which was [[Slave circuit|slaved]] to remote systems in the control ship. Many TIE M5's also had [[Deflector shield generator/Legends|shield]] generators added with no discernable loss of performance.

==History==
[[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] forces first encountered the M5 when the Empire executed a retaliatory strike against the cruiser [[Liberty/Legends|''Liberty'']]. They were also present at the main TIE Experimental research facility, defending the hangar ship ''[[Sardis]]'' and [[Space station/Legends|space station]] [[Obsidian (XQ1 Platform)|''Obsidian'']].

After the destruction of space platform ''Obsidian'', no TIE Experimental fighters were seen again.

==Appearances==
*''[[Star Wars: X-Wing Alliance]]''
